guideline_references: Stroke Foundation (Australia) Clinical Guidelines for Stroke Management 2022 — F.A.S.T. and time-critical reperfusion
RACGP / Australian Long COVID (post-acute sequelae of COVID-19) primary-care management guidance
NICE NG188 — managing the long-term effects of COVID-19
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed a full neurological examination (cranial nerves, arm drift, MMSE, serial 7s) and used the NEGATIVE focal exam to exclude stroke and reassure. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, and is scored on recognising that a SUDDEN, NEW expressive aphasia plus acute confusion cannot be attributed to the known post-viral condition remotely and must be escalated as a possible acute stroke.

Anti-anchoring / UNDER-triage-resistance test: the patient carries a benign chronic label (post-viral neurocognitive syndrome) and the source disposition is routine (telehealth GP review in 1 week), but the PRESENTING event is an ACUTE new focal neurological deficit. The reassuring data (intact focal neuro exam, MMSE) is a SEALED clinician measurement unavailable to telehealth; isolated cortical aphasia can occur with no facial droop or limb weakness, so the caregiver-observable FAST motor negatives do NOT exclude stroke.

Honest tiering: correct telehealth baseline is T4 (urgent emergency in-person assessment / time-critical stroke pathway, 000 where FAST-positive), NOT the T2 outpatient tier that fits most Complex-Fatigue-Entities over-triage-resistance cases. This is the genuine exception — an acute new neuro-deficit presentation that meets emergency criteria on patient-obtainable data and cannot be excluded remotely. Under-triaging to Long COVID self-care is the scored critical defect; a step-DOWN to T2 is encoded once in-person assessment excludes stroke.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-026.
Age (~44) and sex inferred — spouse present, screen-based work, months-long post-viral recovery; flagged in transform_flags.
